title: elaeocarpus ferrugineus subsp. parviflorus
intro: Elaeocarpus ferrugineus subsp. parviflorus, also known as the small-flowered blueberry ash, is a plant species in the family Elaeocarpaceae, characterized by its small white flowers and blue or black berries. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as arthritis, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Elaeocarpus ferrugineus subsp. parviflorus include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system, while its herbal preparations include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, tonics, and salves. By harnessing the medicinal properties of this plant, individuals can potentially alleviate symptoms and improve overall well-being.
